sentences.text: Item/Service Description Prostate Specific Antigen (PSA), a tumor marker for adenocarcinoma of the prostate, can predict residual tumor in the post-operative phase of prostate cancer.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Three to six months after radical prostatectomy, PSA is reported to provide a sensitive indicator of persistent disease.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Six months following introduction of antiandrogen therapy, PSA is reported as capable of distinguishing patients with favorable response from those in whom limited response is anticipated.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 1 of 5 3/10/26, 06:43 NCD - Prostate Specific Antigen (190.31) https://www.cms.gov/medicare-coverage-database/vie... PSA when used in conjunction with other prostate cancer tests, such as digital rectal examination, may assist in the decision making process for diagnosing prostate cancer.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: PSA also, serves as a marker in following the progress of most prostate tumors once a diagnosis has been established.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This test is also an aid in the management of prostate cancer patients and in detecting metastatic or persistent disease in patients following treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Indications and Limitations of Coverage Indications PSA is of proven value in differentiating benign from malignant disease in men with lower urinary tract signs and symptoms (e.g., hematuria, slow urine stream, hesitancy, urgency, frequency, nocturia and incontinence) as well as with patients with palpably abnormal prostate glands on physician exam, and in patients with other laboratory or imaging studies that suggest the possibility of a malignant prostate disorder.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: PSA is also a marker used to follow the progress of prostate cancer once a diagnosis has been established, such as in detecting metastatic or persistent disease in patients who may require additional treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: PSA testing may also be useful in the differential diagnosis of men presenting with as yet undiagnosed disseminated metastatic disease.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Limitations Generally, for patients with lower urinary tract signs or symptoms, the test is performed only once per year unless there is a change in the patient's medical condition.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Testing with a diagnosis of in situ carcinoma is not reasonably done more frequently than once, unless the result is abnormal, in which case the test may be repeated once.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Coding guidance now published in Medicare Lab NCD Manual.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE
